---
title: "How Apple Is Organized for Innovation"
description: "Apple's functional org structure — experts leading experts, immersion in details, collaborative debate — is key to its innovation."
author: synesis
date: "2023-01-13"
categories: [Apple]
image: articleshare.jpg
---

![](articleshare.jpg)

An excellent article by Joel Podolny and Morten Hansen on Harvard Business Review -- How Apple is Organized for Innovation: [[1]](#ref-1)

* Functionally divided vs. product divided.
* Experts leading experts.
* Immersion in details.
* Willingness to collaboratively debate.

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_how-apple-is-organized-for-innovation-activity-7019543476082003968-Qfvj).*

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] Joel Podolny and Morten Hansen. "How Apple Is Organized for Innovation." *Harvard Business Review*, November 2020. <https://hbr.org/2020/11/how-apple-is-organized-for-innovation>
